maml:description.maml:para: Get a test case.

Gets all functional test cases included in a test suite or retrieves a specific test case by its ID. By adding the `-IncludeRecentlyDeleted` switch parameter when retrieving all, the list can also include test cases deleted within the last 30 days, which you can restore.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes items deleted within the last 30 days, which you can restore.
When enabled, returns test cases with resolved variables assigned statically.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes items deleted within the last 30 days, which you can restore.
When enabled, returns test cases with resolved variables assigned statically.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.
Get the test catalog template.
Gets the test catalog template used for creating conditions.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get test conditions.
Gets condition statements you can use for creating test cases.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get test requests.
Gets test requests created in your organization.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get a test run.
Gets all test runs for your test suite or retrieves a specific test run by its ID.
Your test run's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, includes audit information for run test cases and test suites in the response.
When enabled, includes test run context in the response.
When enabled, includes details on objects skipped during the test run in the response.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes audit information for run test cases and test suites in the response.
When enabled, includes test run context in the response.
When enabled, includes details on objects skipped during the test run in the response.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test run's ID.
Get test run's detailed results.
Gets a raw request and response for all URLs in a test run.
Your test run's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test run's ID.
Get a test suite with child objects.
Gets all test suites in your account or retrieves a specific test suite by its ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes child objects such as test cases and variables in the result.
When enabled, returns test cases with resolved variables assigned statically.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes items deleted within the last 30 days, which you can restore.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Filters the list to items created, edited, or deleted by a specific username.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, includes child objects such as test cases and variables in the result.
When enabled, includes items deleted within the last 30 days, which you can restore.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
When enabled, returns test cases with resolved variables assigned statically.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Filters the list to items created, edited, or deleted by a specific username.
Get a variable.
Gets details of a variable used in a test suite.
Your test suite's ID.
A variable's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
A variable's ID.
Generate a test suite with child objects.

Generates a default test suite with test cases and variables for a specific property version and a URL. Based on property settings and its behaviors and the URL, Test Center generates a default test suite object with test cases and variables. You can modify the generated test suite and add it to Test Center using `New-TestSuite`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.
Add test cases to a test suite.

Adds a list of new functional test cases to a test suite. Each test case consists of a test request, client profile, and condition. If you want to use variables within a condition or test request's URL or request header, run the `New-TestVariable` operation first.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Client type you want to use for the test run, either `CHROME` or `CURL`.
Condition expression you want to evaluate in the test run. To create the expression, you need to Get the test catalog template.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Encodes requestBody. It applies only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` to `POST`. Defaults to `true`.
Location type you want to use for the test run. The only currently supported value is US.
IP version to use in the test run, either `IPV4` or `IPV6`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
Request body for the `testRequestUrl`. Provide it only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` is `POST`.

One or more hashtables with the following members: `headerAction` - should always be 'ADD', `headerName` - the name of the header to add, `headerValue` - the value of the header to add. For example `@{ headerAction = 'ADD'; headerName = 'user-agent'; headerValue = 'powershell' }`.
Request method for the testRequestUrl. The available values are: HEAD, GET, and POST.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Lists keywords for the test request. They're useful to filter test cases in the Test Center application.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.
Your test suite's ID.
Variable used in `functionExpression`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Client type you want to use for the test run, either `CHROME` or `CURL`.
Condition expression you want to evaluate in the test run. To create the expression, you need to Get the test catalog template.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Encodes `requestBody`. It applies only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` to `POST`. Defaults to `true`.
Location type you want to use for the test run. The only currently supported value is US.
IP version to use in the test run, either `IPV4` or `IPV6`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
Request body for the `testRequestUrl`. Provide it only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` is `POST`.

One or more hashtables with the following members: headerAction - should always be 'ADD', headerName - the name of the header to add, headerValue - the value of the header to add. For example `@{ headerAction = 'ADD'; headerName = 'user-agent'; headerValue = 'powershell' }`.
Request method for the testRequestUrl. The available values are: HEAD, GET, and POST.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Lists keywords for the test request. They're useful to filter test cases in the Test Center application.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.
Your test suite's ID.
Variable used in `functionExpression`.
Create a test suite.

Creates a new test suite. When running this operation, you can optionally auto-generate the child objects with the `-AutoGenerate` switch parameter. > Note: Your request body will be parsed to check for the presence of test cases or variables and will be routed to the correct endpoint appropriately.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Locks the test suite so that only designated owners or editors can modify it.
Runs the test suite statefully, retaining cookies and session data for subsequent test cases within the test suite.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Description of the test suite, when available.
Unique name for each test suite.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Locks the test suite so that only designated owners or editors can modify it.
Runs the test suite statefully, retaining cookies and session data for subsequent test cases within the test suite.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Description of the test suite, when available.
Unique name for each test suite.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Locks the test suite so that only designated owners or editors can modify it.
Runs the test suite statefully, retaining cookies and session data for subsequent test cases within the test suite.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Description of the test suite, when available.
Unique name for each test suite.
Add variables to a test suite.
Adds new variables to a test suite. You can use variables in a test request's URL or request headers and in condition expression, as a substitute of placeholders.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.

Specifies a variable group referenced in `testRequestUrl`, `headerName`, `headerValue`, `requestBody`, or `conditionExpression` of a test case. Variable groups appear with `{{variableName.columnHeader}}` syntax.
Name for the variable you wish to create.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Name for the variable you wish to create.
Value for the variable you wish to create.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.

Specifies a variable group referenced in `testRequestUrl`, `headerName`, `headerValue`, `requestBody`, or `conditionExpression` of a test case. Variable groups appear with `{{variableName.columnHeader}}` syntax.
Name for the variable you wish to create.
Value for the variable you wish to create.
Remove test cases.
Removes functional test cases with specific identifiers from a test suite. To get the identifiers, run the `Get-TestCase` operation.
Your test suite's ID.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.
Delete a test suite.
Deletes a test suite and the included child objects, such as test cases and variables. You can restore test suites within 30 days of deleting them by running the `Restore-TestSuite` operation.
Your test suite's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Delete variables.
Deletes unused variables from a test suite.
Your test suite's ID.
A variable's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
A variable's ID.
Restore test cases.

Restores functional test cases into a test suite. To get the ID of the test case you want to restore, run the `Get-TestCase` operation with the `-IncludeRecentlyDeleted` switch parameter. With this operation, you can restore only those test cases that were deleted no later than 30 days from their deletion date.
Your test suite's ID.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.
Restore a test suite.
Restores a deleted test suite and the included child objects. With this operation, you can restore only those test suits that were deleted no later than 30 days from their deletion date.
Your test suite's ID.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Update test cases.

Updates functional test cases in a test suite. > Note: Due to the complexity of the request body, it's recommended you retrieve the existing test case with the `Get-TestCase` operation, edit it locally, and pipe the object to this function.
Your test suite's ID.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Reorder test cases in a test suite.

Reorders functional test cases in a test suite. The order of test cases applies when running a test for stateful test suites. You need to provide the target order for all test cases included in a test suite. To check the current order of test cases, run the `Get-TestSuite` operation.
Your test suite's ID.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Update a test suite with child objects.

Updates a test suite with or without included test cases and variables. > Note: Your request body will be parsed to check for the presence of test cases or variables, and routed to the correct endpoint accordingly.
Your test suite's ID.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Update variables.
Updates variables in a test suite.
Your test suite's ID.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
When enabled, return response metadata as well as the usual 'successes' object.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Your test suite's ID.
Start a test run for a Property version.
Submits a test run for functional testing of a property version.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Notes about the test run.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Notes about the test run.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION`or `STAGING`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Notes about the test run.

The ID of the property associated with the test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `-PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyName` to specify the property.

The name of the property associated with a test suite to filter the list by. You can combine this parameter with `- PropertyVersion`. Use either this parameter or `-PropertyID` to specify the property.
The version of the associated property to filter the test suites by. Combine this parameter with `-PropertyID` or `-PropertyName`.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.
Start a test run.
Submits a test run for functional testing of a simple test case.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Client type you want to use for the test run, either `CHROME` or `CURL`.
Condition expression you want to evaluate in the test run. To create the expression, you need to Get the test catalog template.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Encodes `requestBody`. It applies only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` to `POST`. Defaults to `true`.
Location type you want to use for the test run. The only currently supported value is US.
IP version to use in the test run, either `IPV4` or `IPV6`.
Notes about the test run.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
Request body for the `testRequestUrl`. Provide it only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` is `POST`.

One or more hashtables with the following members: headerAction - should always be 'ADD', headerName - the name of the header to add, headerValue - the value of the header to add. For example `@{ headerAction = 'ADD'; headerName = 'user-agent'; headerValue = 'powershell' }`.
Request method for the testRequestUrl. The available values are: HEAD, GET, and POST.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Lists keywords for the test request. They're useful to filter test cases in the Test Center application.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Client type you want to use for the test run, either `CHROME` or `CURL`.
Condition expression you want to evaluate in the test run. To create the expression, you need to Get the test catalog template.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Encodes `requestBody`. It applies only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` to `POST`. Defaults to `true`.
Location type you want to use for the test run. The only currently supported value is US.
IP version to use in the test run, either `IPV4` or `IPV6`.
Notes about the test run.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
Request body for the `testRequestUrl`. Provide it only if `clientType` is set to `CURL` and `requestMethod` is `POST`.

One or more hashtables with the following members: headerAction - should always be 'ADD', headerName - the name of the header to add, headerValue - the value of the header to add. For example `@{ headerAction = 'ADD'; headerName = 'user-agent'; headerValue = 'powershell' }`.
Request method for the testRequestUrl. The available values are: HEAD, GET, and POST.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Lists keywords for the test request. They're useful to filter test cases in the Test Center application.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.

Fully qualified URL of the page or object you want to test. The URL needs to include a protocol, a hostname, a path, and any optional query parameters. Test Center requests this URL from a browser, as well as any requests triggered by it.
Start a test run for a test suite.
Submits a test run for functional testing of a test suite.
Your test suite's ID.
Your functional test case's ID.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.
Notes about the test run.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Notes about the test run.
Purges the configuration for the test run. It applies only for targetEnvironment set to STAGING.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Sends a notification email after the test run completes.
Environment against which the test run executes the request, either `PRODUCTION` or `STAGING`.
Your functional test case's ID.
Your test suite's ID.
Check how functions work.
Runs a created function on sample data to check whether it returns the expected value. The function is valid for use in a test case if the response's `result` value returns only one value.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
One or more strings in the format key=value, e.g. "mycookie=true".
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Function expression you want to test. To learn more about supported functions, see Available functions (ref:variables-overview#available-functions).
HTTP version.
One or more strings in the format key=value, e.g. "content-type=application/json".
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
HTTP status text.
Variable used in `functionExpression`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or string. This parameter supports pipelining.
One or more strings in the format key=value, e.g. "mycookie=true".
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
Function expression you want to test. To learn more about supported functions, see Available functions (ref:variables-overview#available-functions).
HTTP version.
One or more strings in the format key=value, e.g. "content-type=application/json".
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
HTTP status text.
Variable used in `functionExpression`.
